sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the full picture:
- Purple bar at row 63, cols 0-27 (28 pixels) - this is likely a progress/timer bar
- White bar at row 63, cols 28-63 (36 pixels) - this is the remaining portion
- The purple bar seems to grow as I collect green blocks
- Yellow pixel at rows 38-39, col 32 - this is part of the agent (the "eye" or marker)
- Agent (blue, 6 pixels) at rows 37-40, cols 33-34
The purple bar at the bottom is growing! It went from some initial length to 28 pixels. The white bar is 36 pixels. Total is 64 pixels (full width). This is a progress bar!
So the goal IS to collect green blocks, and the purple bar tracks progress. When the purple bar fills up completely (or reaches some threshold), the level completes.
Current state:
- Purple: 28/64 pixels
- White: 36/64 pixels remaining
- 9 green blocks visible
I need to keep collecting green blocks to fill the purple bar. Let me count how many more collections are needed. If each collection adds ~4 pixels (28/7 ≈ 4), I need about 9 more collections (36/4 = 9).
Let me just keep collecting all visible green blocks efficiently. The agent is at col 33-34.
Green blocks visible:
- rows 13-17, cols 31-35 (center 33) - aligned!
- rows 13-17, cols 37-41 (center 39)
- rows 31-35, cols 13-17 (center 15)
- rows 31-35, cols 19-23 (center 21)
- rows 31-35, cols 25-29 (center 27)
- rows 37-41, cols 13-17 (center 15)
- rows 37-41, cols 19-23 (center 21)
- rows 37-41, cols 25-29 (center 27)
- rows 55-59, cols 43-47 (center 45)
Let me collect them efficiently. Agent at col 33.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple bar at bottom is a progress bar! Goal is to fill it by collecting green blocks.
# Purple: 28/64, need to fill remaining 36
# Each collection seems to add ~4 pixels, so need ~9 more collections

# Current agent at col 33-34
# Green blocks at center cols: 33, 39, 15, 21, 27, 15, 21, 27, 45

# Collect efficiently - start with aligned block at col 33
# Then go right to 39, then left through 27, 21, 15 (both rows), then right to 45

actions = [
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 15, 'col': 33},  # Aligned block
    'RIGHT',
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 15, 'col': 39},  # Col 39
    'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT',
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 27},  # Col 27, row 31-35
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 27},  # Col 27, row 37-41
    'LEFT',
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 21},  # Col 21, row 31-35
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 21},  # Col 21, row 37-41
    'LEFT',
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 15},  # Col 15, row 31-35
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 15},  # Col 15, row 37-41
    'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT',
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 45},  # Col 45, bottom
]

result = action(actions)
print(f"Result: done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check progress bar
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        print(f"Purple bar: {n['pixels']} pixels")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 20:
        print(f"White bar: {n['pixels']} pixels")
green_count = sum(1 for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 1)
print(f"Green blocks remaining: {green_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
